assessments.status_rationale: Today’s coverage of China’s NPC/Two Sessions focuses on the growth target (e.g., below 5%) and related macro signals, but the bullets provided do not cite the 2026 official general public budget deficit target or a figure (≥3.5% of GDP) from the Government Work Report/MoF budget report.
The news items discuss heightened U.S.-Iran conflict dynamics, but none indicate that CISA/FBI/NSA issued a joint cyber advisory explicitly attributing critical-infrastructure targeting to Iran.

A MarketWatch item references a ‘Blue Owl’ private-credit stumble, but the prediction is specifically about a large U.S. private-credit interval fund sponsored by Blackstone, Apollo, Ares, or KKR disclosing <90% fulfillment (pro-rated repurchases) in a single period; no such disclosure is evidenced in today’s bullets.

Apple-related news in the bullets concerns the MacBook Neo product announcement; there is no mention of an iOS/iPadOS Rapid Security Response or out-of-band update with ‘exploited in the wild’ plus ‘mercenary spyware’ language.
No earnings release/SEC filing details are provided in today’s bullets indicating Apple reported a year-over-year decline in Mac segment revenue.
Today’s bullets do not include any BEA Personal Income and Outlays release or a core PCE year-over-year print at or above 3.0%.
No bullet indicates that the FOMC has cut the federal funds target range (upper bound) by at least 25 bps.

While there is energy/nuclear coverage (e.g., TerraPower permit), none of the bullets show a DOE announcement/FOA of ≥$200 million in new federal funding specifically for fusion energy R&D or fusion-enabling technologies.
